edibility: Microscopic underground symbiont that does not form edible fruit bodies. Ingested unintentionally through plants and soil, but only in trivial amounts that pose no nutritional value.
taste: No flavor — invisible microscopic organism that lives within plant root cells.
search: Found by scientists and farmers through root staining and microscopy techniques. Spores available in soil can be commercially purchased to inoculate gardens for agricultural benefit; not foraged.

toxicity.symptoms: N/A — not toxic, beneficial to plant agriculture
toxicity.antidote: N/A — not toxic, beneficial to plant agriculture
